items.title: DGSeg: Dynamic Gating of Semantic-Spatial Guided Predictions for Reasoning Segmentation
items.abstract: Reasoning segmentation aims to predict pixel-wise masks for targets given complex language queries. Existing approaches leverage Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) for vision-language reasoning and generate intermediate target cues (e.g., points or boxes) to guide a segmentation model. However, compressing rich reasoning into sparse cues often introduces ambiguity and noise, preventing these cues from accurately preserving the reasoning intent. While multiple complementary cues can enrich target information, existing methods typically feed them jointly into a single segmentation process, allowing ambiguous or erroneous cues to affect the entire prediction. Therefore, we propose DGSeg, a reasoning segmentation framework that learns to fuse predictions guided by semantic and spatial cues. Specifically, the MLLM jointly reasons about both target identity and spatial location, producing complementary semantic and spatial cues that are fed into separate segmentation branches. Their predictions are adaptively integrated by a lightweight dynamic gating module trained with relative branch-quality supervision to suppress noisy or conflicting regions. Extensive experiments demonstrate that DGSeg consistently outperforms strong baselines on multiple benchmarks and achieves 69.6% and 67.3% gIoU on the challenging ReasonSeg validation and test splits. Code is available at https://github.com/RZZeng/DGSeg.
items.llm_evidence_cn: 推理分割用于视觉推理
items.llm_evidence: 推理分割用于视觉推理
items.canonical_evidence: 推理分割用于视觉推理
items.llm_tldr_en: DGSeg proposes dynamic gating of semantic-spatial predictions to improve reasoning segmentation from complex language queries, addressing ambiguity in sparse cues.
items.llm_tldr_cn: 针对现有推理分割方法将丰富推理压缩为稀疏线索导致歧义的问题，该论文提出DGSeg，一种动态门控语义-空间引导预测框架。通过动态融合多个互补线索并加权，减少噪声影响，提升分割精度。实验表明其在多个推理分割基准上取得优异性能。该工作为视觉推理中的细粒度分割提供了新思路。
items.llm_tldr: 针对现有推理分割方法将丰富推理压缩为稀疏线索导致歧义的问题，该论文提出DGSeg，一种动态门控语义-空间引导预测框架。通过动态融合多个互补线索并加权，减少噪声影响，提升分割精度。实验表明其在多个推理分割基准上取得优异性能。该工作为视觉推理中的细粒度分割提供了新思路。

items.title: BVS: Bayesian Visual Search with Multimodal Large Language Model for Fine-grained Perception
items.abstract: While Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) demonstrate impressive general capabilities, they struggle with fine-grained perception in ultra-high-resolution (UHR) images, particularly for tiny objects in cluttered scenes. Existing methods face a dilemma: they either rely on inefficient prior-free scanning, or depend on static prior-driven heuristics that lack posterior correction to rectify initial model biases. To address this, we propose BVS (Bayesian Visual Search), a framework that formulates perception as a global optimization problem over a continuous spatial-scale manifold. Specifically, BVS bridges prior guidance with posterior correction: it utilizes an early-stop attention rollout of MLLM to construct reasoning-aware priors, while employing a scale-aware non-stationary kernel and GP-UCB to dynamically rectify noise and recover missing information in the prior through iterative local observations. We provide theoretical guarantees via sub-linear regret bounds, and extensive experiments demonstrate that BVS significantly outperforms state-of-the-art baselines with a superior trade-off between accuracy and efficiency.
items.llm_evidence_en: fine-grained perception in ultra-high-resolution images for visual reasoning
items.llm_evidence_cn: 超高清图像中的细粒度感知，支持视觉推理
items.llm_evidence: 超高清图像中的细粒度感知，支持视觉推理
items.canonical_evidence: 超高清图像中的细粒度感知，支持视觉推理
items.llm_tldr_en: BVS formulates fine-grained perception as a global optimization over spatial-scale manifold, combining prior guidance with posterior correction for improved MLLM reasoning.
items.llm_tldr_cn: 多模态大模型在处理超高清图像中的小物体时存在困难，现有方法在无先验扫描和静态先验间进退维谷。该论文提出贝叶斯视觉搜索（BVS），将感知建模为连续空间-尺度流形上的全局优化，通过早期停止注意力展开构建推理感知先验，并利用贝叶斯纠偏实现后验校正。在多个细粒度基准上取得最优结果。
items.llm_tldr: 多模态大模型在处理超高清图像中的小物体时存在困难，现有方法在无先验扫描和静态先验间进退维谷。该论文提出贝叶斯视觉搜索（BVS），将感知建模为连续空间-尺度流形上的全局优化，通过早期停止注意力展开构建推理感知先验，并利用贝叶斯纠偏实现后验校正。在多个细粒度基准上取得最优结果。
